oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Update on the Upcoming USDA/APHIS Regulations for Birds Not Bred for Research
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Mock, Adrianne
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: This item originally appeared in AFA Watchbird Volume 50 Number 1 (January 2023).The following is an excerpt from the full PDF link.Update on the Upcoming USDA/APHIS Regulationsby Adrianne Mock AFA Legislative VPAs of February 22, 2023 the new USDA/APHIS regulations will be published in the Federal Register. At the link https://www.federalregister.gov/documents/2022/02/22/2022-03565/standardsfor-birds-not-bred-for-use-in-research-under-the-animal-welfare-act you can find the history of the Animal Welfare Act, the timeline for the new regulations to be published and a summary of the comments received during the 3 call-in comment sessions as well as the extended write-in comment period.We are here now due to lawsuits filed by animal rights extremists, and a judge who ruled that the USDA/APHIS must take action and complete the regulations by a specific date. At this time, we cannot make any more comments/ changes. The most recent information we have available is from the February 2022 document – there have been NO FURTHER updates published since then. While there have been several meetings and discussion sessions, we do not know what (if any) changes have been or will be made.Many organizations and individuals commented, generating over 75 comments during the call-in period and over 10,000 write-in comments. While aviculturists, exhibitors, educators, and many private individuals commented, a VERY LARGE number of comments came from animal rights extremist organizations trying to pass themselves off as animal WELFARE organizations. These included HSUS, PeTA, Avian Welfare Coalition and others. There were also comments from zoological societies and keepers, rehome/rescue groups, waterfowl organizations and falconry/ falconers.Click the PDF link associated with this item to view the complete item as originally published.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: AFA Watchbird; Vol. 50 No. 1 (2023): Watchbird January 2023; 7-9
